caption: This educational presentation focuses on the "Linde – Hampson System" as part of a "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" course. The slides feature a technical schematic of a cooling system with components labeled "Makeup gas" and various flow markers, alongside mathematical formulas. The top-left frame introduces the liquid yield formula: $\frac{\dot{m}_l}{\dot{m}} = y = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{h_1 - h_r}$. As the sequence progresses, a colorful gradient graphic appears within the system's heat exchanger area in the top-right frame. By the bottom-left frame, a new section is added stating, "As seen earlier, the effectiveness is $\varepsilon = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{h_1 - h_g}$". The final frame concludes the derivation with the text, "Rearranging the terms, we have $h_1 = \varepsilon(h_1 - h_g) + h_2$". A footer consistently attributes the content to "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and displays an "NPTEL" logo.
